JOURNAL (current thesis state):
# Journal — Investing Agent

## 1. Market view

Universe mixed-to-soft with unusual insider activity this week — Arnhult's Corem conviction buy opened the Aggressive sleeve for the first time. Cash at 70.8%, six positions across five sectors. Holding pattern into the July earnings cluster (Volvo, Essity, Thule all reporting Jul 23–29; ATRLJ ~Jul 10; CORE-B ~Jul 22). No urgency to deploy further before those prints resolve. Vimian and ASSA remain live but patient.

## 2. Holdings

**HM-B.ST** — Core | Consumer Discretionary | 5.0%
- **Thesis:** Karl-Johan Persson bought ~1.2 BSEK across six transactions in eight days into an 11% drawdown — among the strongest insider signals possible on Swedish Large Cap. Starter position pending fundamental confirmation.
- **Sell if:** Profit warning, gross margin collapse, or confirmed structural market-share loss to Shein/Temu. Not on price weakness alone.
- **Status:** `intact`

**ESSITY-B.ST** — Core | Consumer Staples | 8.0%
- **Thesis:** Exceptional 90-day insider cluster — 512 MSEK, 7 buyers, 0 sellers — led by Industrivärden. Defensive staples compounder at ~14x P/E with 3.4% yield. Q2 print ~Jul 24 is the next adjudicator.
- **Sell if:** Sustained margin deterioration where input costs structurally exceed pricing power, or Q2 shows revenue decline accelerating with margin compression. Not on price weakness alone.
- **Status:** `intact`

**THULE.ST** — Core | Consumer Discretionary | 3.6%
- **Thesis:** Ten insiders including chair, vice chair, CEO, and CFO bought ~14–25 MSEK across two waves, zero sellers. Quality mid-cap franchise 28% off highs; weakness is cyclical not structural. Vice chairman's ~11.6 MSEK personal buy in mid-May confirms conviction post-drawdown.
- **Sell if:** Insider turns net seller, operating margin compresses below ~13%, or Q2 (~Jul 29) reveals genuine earnings deterioration rather than cyclical softness.
- **Status:** `intact`

**VOLV-B.ST** — Core | Industrials | 4.7%
- **Thesis:** Industrivärden deployed ~290 MSEK in a single day near 52-week highs. Net cash ~58 BSEK, ~4% yield, 9.8% operating margin despite -9.5% YoY revenue. Controlling owner buying near highs is a strong forward-earnings endorsement. Q2 ~Jul 24.
- **Sell if:** Industrivärden becomes net seller, profit warning, or Q2 reveals order intake collapse rather than cyclical normalisation. Revenue decline alone is not a sell trigger if margins hold.
- **Status:** `intact`

**ATRLJ-B.ST** — Core | Real Estate | 4.9%
- **Thesis:** Tagehus (controlling family) bought ~30 MSEK into Varma's forced 7%-discount block exit. Ericsson 58k sqm Hagastaden anchor lease provides fundamental confirmation. Starter size only given net debt/equity 101%.
- **Sell if:** Further forced-seller overhang materialises without insider response; Q2 (~Jul 10) reveals deteriorating occupancy/lease renewals; or Ericsson lease proves less economically material than signalled.
- **Status:** `intact`

**CORE-B.ST** — Aggressive | Real Estate | 3.0%
- **Thesis:** Rutger Arnhult (controlling owner/CEO) bought ~110 MSEK personally across two waves into a 50%-off, capitulating leveraged commercial real estate name — clearest Aggressive sleeve signal seen this cycle. FCF positive (678 MSEK R12); this is a leverage/rate-sensitivity story, not a going-concern story. Starter size only given net debt/equity 185%.
- **Sell if:** Dilutive equity raise announced, covenant breach disclosed, or Q2 (~Jul 22) reveals LTV deterioration or occupancy collapse. Arnhult becoming a net seller would be immediate exit.
- **Status:** `intact`

## 3. Watchlist

**VIMIAN.ST** — ~49 MSEK insider cluster (Fidelio Vet + CEO, Jun 12) on a quality animal-health compounder; +17.7% 3m recovery underway. Held back by P/E ~40 and negative FCF. Revisit after Q2 print ~Jul 29 before adding a second Aggressive position.

**ASSA-B.ST** — CEO bought ~35 MSEK across April and June on a quality compounder; stock at 332 vs 315 entry target. Q2 ~Jul 28. Watch for price reaching 315 SEK or post-print entry.

**NOLA-B.ST** — Quality industrial (10.8% op margin, clean balance sheet) at 23% drawdown with CEO transition overhang and shrinking revenue. Screener's "10.8 MSEK insider buy" was misleading — actual disclosed buy only 205k SEK. Revisit after Q2 ~Aug 5 and new CEO's first framing.

**LIFCO-B.ST** — April insider cluster (3 buyers, ~12 MSEK) still valid but stock rallied +10.5% 1m; original 270 SEK anchor stale. Revised entry zone 275–285 SEK. Revisit post-Q2 ~Jul 24 if price resets.

**SCST.ST** — Exceptional 8-buyer / 30 MSEK cluster including strategic peer Grupo Lusiaves. Still extended (+25% above 200MA). Revisit if price pulls back to ~135 SEK or post-Jul 28 earnings.

## 4. Lessons learned / open questions

- **Aggressive sleeve discipline:** CORE-B is the first use of the Aggressive sleeve — correctly sized small (~3%), entered on the clearest possible signal (controlling owner, personal capital, two waves). The sleeve exists for exactly this; the multi-week hesitation to open it was correct patience, not timidity.
- **Correlated real estate exposure:** Both ATRLJ and CORE-B are leveraged Swedish commercial real estate — partially correlated. Combined ~7.9%. Sector cap is 25% but concentration risk is real; no further real estate adds without rebalancing one of these.
- **Screener signal vs disclosed filing:** NOLA-B screener angle ("10.8 MSEK insider buy") did not match FI disclosure (205k SEK). Reinforces standing rule: cross-check every cluster signal against actual filings before acting. This is the same screener hygiene issue flagged last week.
- **Open question:** Four positions report in a 12-day window (ATRLJ ~Jul 10, CORE-B ~Jul 22, Volvo/Essity ~Jul 24, Thule ~Jul 29). Consider whether any pre-print trimming is warranted, or whether current sizes (all ≤8%) provide sufficient cushion across what is effectively one concentrated reporting season.

TODAY'S TRIGGERS (why you were invoked):
Flags on held / watchlist names:
  - [WATCH] THULE.ST (insider_sell): 981k SEK sell on Jun 16 — modest in absolute terms but first seller in an otherwise all-buy cluster; revisit thesis direction at weekly cycle.
  - [WATCH] VIMIAN.ST (other): ~2M SEK in sells today (inc. loan-related disposals) partially offset the 61.8M SEK Jun 16 cluster buys — net picture still positive but selling activity softens the watchlist signal; reassess at weekly cycle.
New-name insider signals (not currently held — you may open these):
  - HEXA-B.ST: significant insider buying in last 2 days — see Analyst note below
  - QLIRO.ST: significant insider buying in last 2 days — see Analyst note below
  - BIM.ST: significant insider buying in last 2 days — see Analyst note below
Material news today (>=M3):
  - HM-B.ST: M3 H&M Group subsidiary reports 47% sales increase following store debut

ANALYST NOTES on new (not-yet-held) names surfaced today:
# Hexagon (HEXA-B.ST) — Analyst note

**Thesis.** A cluster of insider buying from multiple executives (CFO + three other senior managers, ~12.4 MSEK over 6 weeks, zero sells) has landed into a stock that's down ~33% from its 52-week high and ~20% below its 200-day MA. When a CFO and several operating leaders buy in size on the way down, it's usually worth a serious look — they see something the tape doesn't. Whether that "something" is enough to act on, I cannot confirm from the data I have.

**Positives**
- Insider cluster is unusually clean: 4 distinct buyers, 0 sellers, 90-day net buy of 12.4 MSEK, all non-equity-program. The CFO's ~3 MSEK purchase on 2026-05-04 is the standout signal.
- News confirms a CEO insider purchase on 2026-06-17 — i.e. an additional buyer beyond what's in the 90d table, reinforcing the cluster.
- Bolt-on M&A activity continues (ITRES acquisition, airborne mapping) — management is acting, not retreating.
- Price is deeply oversold vs trend (-20% vs 200MA, -32% vs 52w high), so any positive surprise into the 2026-07-25 earnings print has room to work.

**Concerns**
- The reported fundamentals look **broken or mis-scaled**: P/E 0.9, FCF yield 154%, dividend yield 20.6%, revenue 55.4 BEUR for Hexagon are not credible numbers. I cannot trust the valuation read from this row and flag it for the PM to verify before any sizing decision.
- Setting the bad data aside: a stock down 33% with multiple insiders buying often means the *next* print is the catalyst — and earnings are ~37 days out. Buying before that print is taking event risk.
- 30-day vol at 82.8% is very high for a large-cap industrial — something is moving this name hard, and I don't have the news flow to know what.
- No prior dossier; I have no view on Hexagon's longer-term thesis (organic growth, software mix, Chinese exposure, the well-known short-seller history) — all of which matter here.

**Insider read.** Strong, multi-person, multi-week buying cluster with zero offsetting sales, including CFO and (per news) CEO. This is the cleanest type of insider signal we look for and the main reason to engage with this name at all.

**Price read.** Deeply oversold — well below 200MA, near 52w lows, high realised vol. Classic "insiders buying weakness" setup, but also classic "knife not yet caught" setup.

**Verdict.** The insider signal is genuinely strong, but I'm missing the news context that would explain the drawdown and I don't trust the fundamentals row as printed. The honest call is to watch into the July 25 print rather than buy blind.

`WATCH — not actionable now, keep on watchlist`

```json

  "verdict": "WATCH — not actionable now, keep on watchlist",

  "key_positive": "Clean multi-executive insider buying cluster (CFO + CEO + 3 other managers, ~12.4 MSEK net, zero sells) into a 33% drawdown.",
  "key_concern": "Reported fundamentals (P/E 0.9, FCF yield 154%, div yield 20.6%) are not credible and need verification; also, Q2 earnings in ~37 days is the likely catalyst, so buying now is taking event risk without knowing what spooked the stock.",
  "would_buy_at": 75.0,
  "would_sell_at": null,

(2) The real decision point is the 2026-07-25 print. Suggested plan: keep on watchlist, re-analyse with news context post-earnings; if the print is clean and insiders haven't sold, that's the entry. Only reason to act pre-print would be conviction that the insider cluster alone is enough — I'm not there with one data cut and no dossier."
}
```

# Qliro (QLIRO.ST) — Analyst Note

**Thesis.** Qliro is a small-cap Swedish payments/BNPL player going through a leadership reset, with a new CEO who has been buying stock heavily in the open market alongside (smaller) sells — a net-buy pattern that, combined with revenue stabilizing and an external research house (ABG) putting a midpoint fair value near current price, makes this an interesting *speculative* situation. But the business is still loss-making at every level (operating margin -20%, FCF -162 MSEK on 431 MSEK revenue), so this is a turnaround bet, not a quality compounder.

**Positives**
- CEO Christoffer Rutgersson has been an active net buyer over 90 days (net +2.25 MSEK, gross buys 4.3 MSEK across multiple dates including a large cluster on 2026-06-16) — meaningful on a sub-200 MSEK market cap name.
- Strategic reset underway: new CEO publicly signaling sharp focus (DI, 2026-06-18) and a new Deputy CEO just appointed — concrete management actions, not just words.
- ABG commissioned research initiated with fair value 15–35 SEK, bracketing today's 18.90 — at least one external party sees credible upside scenarios.
- Net debt is zero, so the company has runway to execute the turnaround without immediate refinancing risk.

**Concerns**
- Business is deeply unprofitable: operating margin -20%, net margin -19%, ROE -14%, FCF yield -30%. Without a clear path to profitability the equity story doesn't work.
- 3-year revenue CAGR is *negative* (-1.3%); the +3.7% YoY recovery is from a low base and one data point.
- I cannot evaluate unit economics, take-rate trends, merchant churn, or competitive position vs. Klarna/Walley from the data I have — these are the variables that actually decide whether the turnaround works.
- The CEO's pattern includes sizable sells alongside buys on the same day (2026-06-16), which is unusual — possibly tax/option-related rather than a clean conviction signal. Net is positive but the signal is muddier than a clean buy cluster.
- ABG research is *commissioned* (paid for by the company), so the 15–35 range should be treated as a sell-side scenario menu, not independent validation.

**Insider read.** Net CEO buying of ~2.25 MSEK over 90 days on a name this size is a real positive signal, particularly the 2026-06-03 and earlier dates which look like clean buys. The 2026-06-16 mixed buy/sell cluster is messier and may be program-adjacent despite the filter — I'd weight the cleaner earlier buys more heavily.

**Price read.** At 18.90 SEK the stock is 26% off its 52w high, roughly flat on 200dma, and modestly positive over 12m — no momentum signal either way. Vol 30% is normal for a small-cap. Price sits at the low end of ABG's fair value range.

**Verdict.** Interesting situation but unprofitable business + thin information edge = not a Core fit, and the conviction isn't high enough for a sized Aggressive bet today. Better to wait for the Q2 report (~Aug 11) to see whether the new CEO's strategic focus is showing in the numbers.

**Verdict: WATCH — not actionable now, keep on watchlist**

```json
{
  "ticker": "QLIRO.ST",
  "name": "Qliro",
  "verdict": "WATCH — not actionable now, keep on watchlist",
  "sleeve_fit": "aggressive",
  "conviction": 2,
  "key_positive": "New CEO is a meaningful net buyer (+2.25 MSEK / 90d) alongside a visible strategic reset on a sub-200 MSEK market cap name.",
  "key_concern": "Business is deeply unprofitable (op margin -20%, FCF -162 MSEK) with negative 3y revenue CAGR; turnaround is unproven and I lack unit-economics data to handicap it.",
  "would_buy_at": 16.0,
  "would_sell_at": 28.0,
  "notes_for_pm": "Re-evaluate after Q2 report (~2026-08-11) — that's the first numbers print under the new CEO and the cleanest read on whether the strategic focus is translating into margin progress. If buying before then, size small (Aggressive sleeve, well under the 10% cap) and treat as a CEO-conviction bet, not a fundamentals bet. Note: ABG research is commissioned by the company — discount accordingly."
}
```

# BIMobject (BIM.ST) — Analyst note

**Thesis.** A cluster of insider buying from two distinct vehicles (Saivo Capital, Liger Invest) absorbed EQT's full exit on 2026-05-26 — a classic forced-seller setup on a First North SaaS name where the overhang has now cleared. Price has bounced ~15% in the last month but is still down 34% over 12 months and well below 52w highs. Fundamentals are weak (operating margin -35%, FCF negative), so this is a sentiment/flow trade on a still-unprofitable company, not a quality story.

**Positives**
- Net buy of ~74.5 MSEK over 90 days from two independent insider vehicles — meaningful conviction on a sub-200 MSEK revenue name.
- The buying coincided exactly with EQT's exit, suggesting the new holders took the block — overhang likely cleared.
- Net cash position (~153 MSEK) gives runway despite negative FCF (~30 MSEK/yr burn → ~5 years).
- Gross margin ~48% on a SaaS-like model leaves a path to profitability if opex is controlled.

**Concerns**
- Operating margin -35%, net margin -30%, ROE -34%, FCF negative — the underlying business is not working at current cost structure.
- Revenue -1.7% YoY with 3y CAGR only +7.7%: growth is decelerating, not a hyper-growth SaaS story.
- First North, illiquid, vol30d ~59% — position sizing must be small.
- EQT's exit signals a sophisticated institutional holder gave up; the insider buyers may be right, but they may also be founders/insiders defending the equity story.
- I cannot evaluate cash burn trajectory, customer churn, or the latest quarterly commentary from the data I have — Q1 was reported 2026-05-05 but I don't have the contents. Next earnings ~Aug 4 is the next real test.

**Insider read.** Strong positive signal — two distinct buyers, large size, perfectly timed against a forced seller. This is the kind of insider cluster the constitution flags as a strong signal on Small/Mid Cap. The Saivo round-trip (sell 33.7M then buy 67.5M same day) looks like a block-trade mechanic, not genuine selling.

**Price read.** Price ~4.84, sitting near the 200-day MA (-2.6%) and -34% from 52w high. The 1m/3m bounce of ~15% suggests the EQT overhang clearing has already started to work; entering here means chasing some of that move. Pre-EQT-exit lows would have been a cleaner entry.

**Verdict.** `INTERESTING — fits Aggressive sleeve`

This is exactly the kind of name the Aggressive sleeve exists for: unprofitable First North story with a clean catalyst (overhang removed + insider cluster). But it is not a Core name and conviction should be modest given I cannot see the operating trajectory. Small size only, and the Aug 4 earnings is the make-or-break event.

```json

  "ticker": "BIM.ST",

  "key_positive": "~74.5 MSEK net insider buying from two distinct vehicles absorbed EQT's full exit on 2026-05-26 — a clean overhang-clearing event on a First North name.",
  "key_concern": "Underlying business is unprofitable (op margin -35%, FCF negative, revenue declining) and I cannot assess the operating trajectory from price data alone — Aug 4 earnings is the real test.",
  "would_buy_at": 4.50,
  "would_sell_at": 7.00,
  "notes_for_pm": "Aggressive sleeve only, small size (≤3% of portfolio given First North illiquidity and 59% vol). Bounce is partly already in price. Key date: Q2 earnings ~2026-08-04 — if op losses haven't narrowed, thesis weakens fast. The Saivo same-day sell/buy looks like a block-trade mechanic, not genuine round-trip."
}
```

RISK CAPS (mirrors constitution §4 — enforced in code):
  - Max 15% single holding (of total portfolio)
  - Max 25% any single sector (of total)
  - Max 10% single Aggressive position
  - Max 20% Aggressive sleeve total
  - Min 30% cash buffer of the Core sleeve
  - Max 90% total equity exposure
  - Max ~10 holdings
  - Min 4-week holding period — early full exit requires thesis_break=True + rationale
response_text:
## Trigger read

Three things landed today: (1) a modest 981k SEK insider sell on THULE — first seller in an otherwise clean cluster, but tiny in context of the prior ~14–25 MSEK buying; (2) ~2M SEK of Vimian sells partially offsetting the Jun 16 cluster — net signal still positive; and (3) three new-name insider clusters where the Analyst said WATCH, WATCH, and INTERESTING-but-small-with-Aug-4-as-the-test.

None of this is news in the §3 sense. THULE's chair, vice-chair, CEO and CFO are not selling — one minor seller does not break a 10-buyer cluster. Vimian remains a watchlist name, not held. The H&M subsidiary 47% sales news is positive but cosmetic; not thesis-relevant.

## Decision

**No trades.** Walking through each candidate honestly:

- **THULE** — sell-if trigger is "insider turns net seller" or margin/earnings deterioration. One 981k SEK sale against ~14–25 MSEK of buys is not a net seller turn. Thesis intact. Journal Keeper can note this on Sunday.
- **VIMIAN** — already on watchlist pending Q2 (~Jul 29); the softening signal is a reason to *not* hurry, not a reason to act. Confirms patience.
- **HEXA-B** — Analyst explicitly said WATCH, flagged broken fundamentals data, and earnings is 37 days out. Buying blind into a -33% drawdown without knowing what spooked the stock is exactly the kind of trade the constitution warns against. Sunday can't help either — needs verification, not speed.
- **QLIRO** — Analyst conviction 2, deeply unprofitable, would_buy_at 16.0 vs 18.90 today. Not actionable on any timeframe.
- **BIM.ST** — the most interesting of the three, and the Analyst tagged it INTERESTING for the Aggressive sleeve. But: (a) Analyst's would_buy_at is 4.50 vs 4.84 today — already chasing the bounce; (b) Aug 4 earnings is the real test, 47 days out, no urgency; (c) opening a second Aggressive name on the same day as CORE-B was opened (Jun 14, four days ago) would mean two unprofitable/leveraged Aggressive names back-to-back with no time to see how CORE-B trades. Sunday is a fine time to revisit this with a fresh Analyst look.

None of this passes "Sunday is too late."

## Risk awareness

Real-estate concentration (ATRLJ + CORE-B = 7.8%) remains the live constraint flagged in the journal — relevant to any future BIM-style add only insofar as we'd want sector diversification, not real-estate. Cash at 70.9% is fine; the July earnings cluster is the right time to redeploy, not today.

```json
{
  "summary": "No action — today's flags are noise (one minor THULE seller, modest Vimian sells) and the three new clusters are all WATCH or pre-earnings; nothing rises above the intra-week bar.",
  "trades": [],
  "no_action_note": "THULE's single 981k sell does not flip the 10-buyer cluster; Vimian remains watchlist; HEXA needs fundamentals verification and Jul 25 print; QLIRO is above Analyst's buy level on conviction 2; BIM is past Analyst's 4.50 entry and Aug 4 earnings is 47 days out. All can wait for Sunday."
}
```
